---
# Geo-locate destination IPs appending a suffix to every geo column

**Dataset**: `gx_11_ext`

When you want every geo column kept but disambiguated from existing fields, `iploc dst_ip suffix=_geo` appends `_geo` to all of the emitted geo columns at once — `loc_country` becomes `loc_country_geo`, `loc_city` becomes `loc_city_geo`, and so on. This is the cleanest way to enrich a row without manually renaming each column and avoids collisions when the dataset already carries `loc_*` fields. The `suffix=` option applies uniformly across the whole geo column set, unlike per-column `as` renames which target only the columns you name.

```sql
dataset = gx_11_ext
| filter dst_ip != null
| iploc dst_ip suffix=_geo
| fields dst_ip, loc_country_geo, loc_city_geo
| limit 20
```
